issues.title: ContextMenu has no ARIA role, no keyboard trap, no Escape-to-close, no focus management
issues.description: The context menu div has no role='menu', menu items have no role='menuitem', there is no keyboard event handler (Escape to close, ArrowUp/Down to navigate), and focus is never moved into the menu when it opens — making it completely inaccessible to keyboard and screen-reader users.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component function: BEFORE: The floating menu div has no ARIA role and the button items inside have no role='menuitem'. There is no keyboard event listener for Escape or arrow keys, and focus is never programmatically moved to the first menu item when the menu opens. AFTER: (1) Add role='menu' and aria-label='Context menu' to the outer menu div. (2) Add role='menuitem' and tabIndex={0} to each button inside the menu. (3) Add a useEffect that, when open becomes true, calls menuRef.current?.querySelector<HTMLButtonElement>('[role="menuitem"]')?.focus() to move focus to the first item. (4) Add an onKeyDown handler on the menu div that calls setOpen(false) when e.key === 'Escape', moves focus to the previous item on ArrowUp, and moves focus to the next item on ArrowDown, calling e.preventDefault() for all three keys. (5) Add aria-haspopup='menu' to the wrapper div that receives onContextMenu. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FolderBreadcrumbs JSX is truncated — file ends mid-expression causing a parse/build error
issues.description: The FolderBreadcrumbs component's return JSX is cut off after the ternary `isLast ? (` with no closing branch, no closing tags, and no export. This will produce a fatal TypeScript/TSX parse error and prevent the entire module from building.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component function: BEFORE: The map callback contains an incomplete ternary expression `isLast ? (` with no false branch, no closing parenthesis, no closing JSX tags for the Fragment or nav, and no closing brace for the component — the file is syntactically broken. AFTER: Complete the ternary so that when isLast is true it renders a non-interactive span showing segment.name styled with fontSize: typography.fontSize.sm, color: colors.textPrimary, and fontWeight 500; and when isLast is false it renders a button using crumbButtonStyle with onClick={() => onNavigate(i)} and text content of segment.name, with the same onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave hover handlers used on the root 'All Files' button. Close the React.Fragment, close the map, close the nav element, and close the component function with its closing brace. Ensure the component is exported as a named export. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ContextMenu items keyed by array index — unstable keys cause React reconciliation bugs on dynamic menus
issues.description: Every menu item and its optional divider are keyed by array index `i`. When items are added, removed, or reordered (e.g. context-sensitive menus per row), React will mis-reconcile focused buttons and fire wrong onClick handlers.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component function: BEFORE: The ContextMenuItem interface has no id field, and the items.map callback uses the array index i as the React key: key={i}. AFTER: Add an optional id field to the ContextMenuItem interface — `id?: string` — then change the map key to: key={item.id ?? `${item.label}-${i}`}. This uses the stable label string as a fallback when no explicit id is provided, preventing reconciliation bugs when the items array changes between renders. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FolderBreadcrumbs has no overflow handling — long folder paths break layout on mobile viewports
issues.description: The nav container uses flexWrap: 'wrap' but individual crumb buttons have no maxWidth or text-overflow truncation. On mobile (< 375px), a deeply nested path like Projects > Preconstruction > Drawings > Revisions overflows its container or wraps into three lines, destroying the header layout.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the crumbButtonStyle constant and the FolderBreadcrumbs component function: BEFORE: crumbButtonStyle has no maxWidth, overflow, textOverflow, or whiteSpace properties, and the nav element has no minWidth:0 constraint. The folder name text can grow unbounded. AFTER: Add to crumbButtonStyle the following properties: maxWidth: '160px', overflow: 'hidden', textOverflow: 'ellipsis', whiteSpace: 'nowrap'. Also add a title={segment.name} attribute to each non-last segment button element so the full name is visible on hover. Add minWidth: 0 to the nav's style object so it respects its flex parent's boundary. For the last (non-interactive) span, apply the same maxWidth, overflow, textOverflow, and whiteSpace values inline. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ContextMenu does not clamp position to viewport — menu renders off-screen on right edge and bottom edge
issues.description: The menu is positioned at the raw clientX/clientY of the right-click event with no check against window.innerWidth or window.innerHeight. On a 1280px screen, right-clicking a row in the bottom-right quadrant will render the 180px-wide menu partially or fully off-screen.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the handleContextMenu callback function: BEFORE: setPosition is called with the raw { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY } values from the mouse event, without any adjustment for viewport boundaries. The menu width (minWidth 180px) and approximate height are not considered. AFTER: Before calling setPosition, compute clamped coordinates: const MENU_WIDTH = 188; const MENU_HEIGHT = items.length * 36 + 8; const clampedX = e.clientX + MENU_WIDTH > window.innerWidth ? e.clientX - MENU_WIDTH : e.clientX; const clampedY = e.clientY + MENU_HEIGHT > window.innerHeight ? e.clientY - MENU_HEIGHT : e.clientY; then call setPosition({ x: clampedX, y: clampedY }). This prevents the menu from rendering outside the visible viewport in both axes. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore and Fieldwire both ship accessible context menus with full keyboard navigation — a basic expectation for enterprise tools. The broken JSX in Breadcrumbs.tsx means any page using folder navigation is currently unshippable, a gap that would be embarrassing relative to even legacy competitors. Fieldwire's mobile-first reputation is built partly on pixel-perfect overflow handling on small screens; the missing viewport clamping and text truncation issues directly undercut SiteSync's field-first positioning.
summary: The UI Design System module has two files with issues severe enough to block production: Breadcrumbs.tsx has a fatal JSX truncation that makes the component unparseable, and ContextMenu.tsx has zero ARIA attributes, no keyboard navigation, and no viewport clamping — making it both inaccessible and visually broken on edge-positioned rows. TypeScript quality is weakened by index-based React keys on a dynamic menu component. Mobile responsiveness is missing the basic overflow/truncation logic needed for nested folder paths on iPhone-sized screens. None of these issues require new backend APIs; all five are precise, surgical fixes that unblock a build error, meet WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard requirements, and harden the component for real field use on construction sites.
